prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
1. Verily, in this Q3 of the year of Our Lord 2023, I, Brother Martin the Scrivener, do recount the marvels and miracles which hath transpired at the mysterious abode of SaaS & Co. Theлле has been much ado about something called "ARR" and "MRR" that doth confound mine head as much as the Holy Trinity confounded the ancient Celtic druids.
2. It doth baffle mine mind, the chatter of "annualized run rate" and "net revenue retention" that doth fill the air of SaaS & Co. like incense in a thy사이트d cathedral. What hath God's servants wrought, to conjure such miracles from the ether of distant digital realms?
3. Lo and behold, the faithful "onboarders" and "solution engineers" did lead the charge against the dire "churn rate" and "conversion funnel" abominations, their pixelated banners waving bravely in the Christ-sent broadband wind.
4. In closed chambers, the wise sages of SaaS & Co., clad in garments of black and white, didst mutter in arcane tongues of "multiples" and "burn rates" whilst staring into the glowing crystal balls of "cash runway" and "EBITDA." Verily, they doth perform miracles on par with loaves and fishes, wringing seemingly limitless gold from sources unseen by mortal eyes.
